metadata.archetype_justification: Prowlarr owns persistent state in SQLite/PostgreSQL databases, exposes full CRUD operations on business entities (indexers, applications, tags, notifications), and manages user-specific configuration data.

findings.title: No machine identity authentication — single shared API key
findings.description: Prowlarr uses a single shared API key for all programmatic access. The same API key is used by all consumers — there is no mechanism to issue distinct machine identities per agent or per consumer. Authentication validates possession of the key but cannot distinguish which agent instance made a call.
findings.gap: No support for per-agent machine identity. A single shared API key cannot provide principal attribution in audit logs.
findings.recommendation: Implement multiple API key support with principal labels. Each agent or integration gets its own key that is logged with its label/name in the Auth logger.

blocker_remediation.immediate: Implement multiple API key support with principal labels.
blocker_remediation.target_state: Each agent identity has a unique credential with attribution in audit logs, and individual keys can be revoked without affecting other consumers.

findings.title: No scoped permissions — binary access model
findings.description: The application has no scoped permission model. Authentication is binary — a valid API key grants full access to all endpoints and all operations (read, write, delete). There is no concept of roles, scopes, or permission levels.
findings.gap: No least-privilege enforcement. An agent with the API key can perform any operation including destructive ones.
findings.recommendation: Implement role-based access control (RBAC) with at least read-only and admin roles. Add scope claims to API keys.

findings.title: No action-level authorization
findings.description: No action-level authorization exists. Once authenticated, there is no distinction between read and write operations. A caller with the API key can GET, POST, PUT, and DELETE on any resource.
findings.gap: Cannot restrict an agent to read-only operations at the application level.
findings.recommendation: Introduce an authorization policy that distinguishes read (GET) from write (POST/PUT/DELETE) operations, enforceable per authenticated principal.

findings.title: No immutable audit logging for API operations
findings.description: Authentication events are logged locally. However, there is no immutable audit trail for API operations. Logs are written to local rotating files with no integrity protection.
findings.gap: No immutable, tamper-evident audit log for API operations. No centralized audit logging. API operation logs do not record which principal performed the action.
findings.recommendation: Add audit logging middleware that logs principal identity, HTTP method, resource path, and timestamp for all state-changing operations. Ship logs to an immutable store.

ara_metadata.conditional_resolution: Resolved to RISK-SAFETY: agent_scope=read-only, audit logging still important but not a deployment blocker.
ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: Read-only agents do not execute write operations, reducing but not eliminating the need for immutable audit trails.

findings.title: No per-agent identity suspension capability
findings.description: There is only one API key for the entire application. It cannot be selectively revoked for one agent without revoking access for all consumers.
findings.gap: All-or-nothing key revocation. Cannot isolate a misbehaving agent without disrupting all integrations.
findings.recommendation: Implement multi-key support where individual keys can be disabled without affecting others. Requires AUTH-Q1 to be resolved first.

findings.title: No server-side rate limiting on API endpoints
findings.description: There is no server-side rate limiting on the Prowlarr API. The existing RateLimitService is client-side only — it rate-limits outbound requests to external indexers, not inbound API calls.
findings.gap: A runaway agent loop could overwhelm the application at machine speed. No protection against brute-force or denial-of-service.
findings.recommendation: Add ASP.NET Core rate limiting middleware (Microsoft.AspNetCore.RateLimiting) or deploy an API Gateway with throttling.

findings.title: Sensitive credentials exposed in API responses
findings.description: HostConfigResource returns ApiKey, SslCertPassword, ProxyUsername, and ProxyPassword directly in the API response. No field-level filtering for sensitive data.
findings.gap: Agent-facing API responses include plaintext secrets. No DTO separation for sensitive vs non-sensitive data.
findings.recommendation: Create separate response DTOs for sensitive config endpoints that exclude credential fields. Mark sensitive fields with [JsonIgnore].

ara_metadata.conditional_resolution: Resolved to RISK-SAFETY: agent_scope=read-only, B1 fires as RISK-SAFETY because sensitive fields are returned in general API responses under read-only scope.
ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: DATA-Q1 B1 fired because HostConfigResource returns ApiKey, SslCertPassword, ProxyPassword in standard API responses. Under read-only scope, this is RISK-SAFETY rather than BLOCKER.

b1.reasoning: Sensitive fields (ApiKey, SslCertPassword, ProxyPassword) are returned in the GET /api/v1/config/host response. Agent scope is read-only, so RISK-SAFETY rather than BLOCKER.

b2.reasoning: No access control differentiation — single API key grants access to all endpoints including sensitive config.

b3.reasoning: No formal classification metadata exists. No field-level sensitivity annotations.

findings.title: Reactive PII redaction model in logs
findings.description: Comprehensive 40+ regex pattern redaction applied to all log outputs. However, this is a reactive pattern-based approach rather than proactive allowlisting. Novel credential formats may not be captured.
findings.gap: Reactive pattern-based redaction rather than proactive field-allowlisting. New credential formats may not be covered.
findings.recommendation: Migrate to allowlist-based logging model for sensitive contexts. Ensure new indexer credential fields are automatically covered.

findings.title: OpenAPI spec lacks CI validation for drift
findings.description: An OpenAPI 3.0.4 JSON specification exists and is committed. Generated via Swashbuckle. However, there is no CI step that validates spec-code synchronization.
findings.gap: No automated spec drift detection in CI pipeline.
findings.recommendation: Add a CI step that generates the OpenAPI spec and fails if it differs from the committed version.

findings.title: Error responses lack retryable classification
findings.description: Global error pipeline maps exceptions to HTTP codes with structured JSON bodies. Lacks retryable indicator and machine-readable error codes beyond HTTP status.
findings.gap: No retryable/terminal classification in error responses. No standardized machine-readable error codes.
findings.recommendation: Add error code and retryable boolean to error response envelope.

findings.title: No breaking change detection in CI
findings.description: API versioned at URL level (/api/v1/). OpenAPI spec committed. No breaking change detection, no consumer-driven contract tests.
findings.gap: No automated breaking change detection. Agent tool schemas could break silently.
findings.recommendation: Add OpenAPI diff tooling (oasdiff) and contract tests to CI.

findings.title: No distributed tracing or correlation IDs
findings.description: No OpenTelemetry, X-Ray, or trace ID propagation. Logging uses pipe-delimited format by default. Optional CLEF JSON available. No correlation IDs.
findings.gap: Cannot trace agent-initiated requests. No structured logging by default. No request correlation.
findings.recommendation: Add OpenTelemetry instrumentation and correlation ID middleware. Enable structured JSON logging.

findings.title: No alerting for API error rates or latency
findings.description: No alerting configured for API error rates or latency. Custom health check system monitors indexer connectivity but not API performance.
findings.gap: No automated detection of API degradation from agent traffic.
findings.recommendation: Add metrics export (Prometheus) and alert thresholds for 5xx rates and P99 latency.

findings.title: No infrastructure as code in repository
findings.description: No IaC exists. Application distributed as self-contained archives deployed manually or via community packages.
findings.gap: No IaC governance for deployment infrastructure. No drift detection, no peer review on infrastructure changes.
findings.recommendation: Create IaC definitions for deployment infrastructure externally — at minimum networking, reverse proxy, and access control.

findings.title: No API contract testing in CI/CD
findings.description: Comprehensive CI/CD pipeline exists with unit and integration tests. No API contract tests or breaking change detection.
findings.gap: Breaking API changes not caught in CI. Agent tool bindings could break silently.
findings.recommendation: Add contract testing (Pact or OpenAPI diff) to Azure Pipelines.

findings.title: Manual file-system-level rollback only
findings.description: Self-update mechanism backs up current installation before applying updates. No platform-level rollback, no blue/green, no canary, no automated rollback triggers.
findings.gap: Manual rollback only. No deployment platform with automated rollback capability.
findings.recommendation: Containerize and deploy via orchestration with automated rollback on health check failure.

findings.title: No encryption at rest for local data store
findings.description: SQLite plain files with no encryption. Credentials stored in database accessible with filesystem access. No KMS integration.
findings.gap: No encryption at rest for the local SQLite database containing indexer credentials.
findings.recommendation: Deploy on encrypted volumes or use SQLCipher for SQLite encryption.

findings.title: Offset-only pagination without cursor support
findings.description: Page-based offset pagination with page/pageSize/sort parameters. No cursor-based pagination, no field selection, no enforced maximum page size.
findings.gap: Offset pagination degrades at scale. No cursor-based pagination. No field selection for payload reduction.
findings.recommendation: Add maximum page size enforcement and consider cursor-based pagination for large collections.

evaluations.rationale: Well-documented REST API at /api/v1/ with 31 controllers and committed OpenAPI 3.0.4 specification.
